name: Progressive Majority
subject: Nearly a third of Democrats polled might STAY HOME on Election Day in November
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Progressive Majority PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hi Peter \-- The day after Trump's acquittal, he took to national television to defend his "perfect call" with Ukraine and attacked Adam Schiff, calling him a "vicious, horrible person." He declared the impeachment "evil," calling it "corrupt" and full of "dirty cops," "leakers and liars." And now he is retaliating against national security official Lt. Col. Vindman because of his testimony during the impeachment trial. But according to a recent Emerson poll, 13% of Democrats say they won't vote against Trump if their top Democratic candidate doesn't win the primary. And another 15% say it depends on who becomes the nominee. If nearly a third of Democrats stay HOME in November, Trump will be re-elected without a check on his presidency. We need to know where you stand tonight: | | **Will you vote in November to defeat Donald Trump?**
[ Yes
No](https://act.progressivemajoritypac.com/page/m/3f9a5c64/5360b1c8/2d74ff65/b0712f7/2476121232/VEsH/?g=RdxxUK0LBwxeO7oKxQx0RTA "https://s.surveyplanet.com/0SqhGUyN") |
---|---|---

Thank you for your response,
Progressive Majority

clean_body: | | | Hi Peter \-- The day after Trump's acquittal, he took to national television to defend his "perfect call" with Ukraine and attacked Adam Schiff, calling him a "vicious, horrible person." He declared the impeachment "evil," calling it "corrupt" and full of "dirty cops," "leakers and liars." And now he is retaliating against national security official Lt. Col. Vindman because of his testimony during the impeachment trial. But according to a recent Emerson poll, 13% of Democrats say they won't vote against Trump if their top Democratic candidate doesn't win the primary. And another 15% say it depends on who becomes the nominee. If nearly a third of Democrats stay HOME in November, Trump will be re-elected without a check on his presidency. We need to know where you stand tonight: | | Will you vote in November to defeat Donald Trump? name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Trump has Given Us a Path to a Balanced Budget – It's Pro-Worker, Pro-Family and Pro-Growth
body:
"In 2017, Speaker Pelosi famously cajoled House Republicans to produce a budget, warning ‘show me your budget, show me your values.’" | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fdd764f322&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Trump has Given Us a Path to a Balanced Budget – It's Pro-Worker, Pro-Family and Pro-Growth**

“In 2017, Speaker Pelosi famously cajoled House Republicans to produce a budget, warning ‘show me your budget, show me your values.’ She was understood to mean that you can learn about someone’s deepest convictions judging the way in which they spend their money,” Rep. Jim Banks (R-IN) writes for Fox News.

“Ironically, she and her party never produced a budget when they regained control of the House in 2019 . . . In contrast, President Trump is not afraid to put his cards on the table.” His new proposed budget is a stark departure from Beltway “business as usual,” putting the Federal ledger on a path back to surpluses in just 15 years.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e8a724ffd2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| The "petulant" antics of House Speaker Nancy Pelosi during last week’s State of the Union address were not only a poor reflection on her—they showed Congressional Democrats’ true colors. It "underscored the desperation of a party that has lurched to the left" to satisfy its increasingly socialist base, Miranda Devine writes in the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=978b13d132&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| House Democrats wasted 178 days on their impeachment charade. “The vote for acquittal in the Senate does not negate the fact that the do-nothing Democrats wasted three years and millions of taxpayer dollars by supporting baseless witch hunts against a president they have wanted to destroy since day one,” Steve Pearce writes in the [_Albuquerque Journal_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4f5fb21ce3&e=3ee4985eea "https://www.abqjournal.com/1418600/178-wasted-days-will-hurt-democrats.html").
---
|
---
| “Today, after months of impeachment proceedings, accusations of wrongdoing and a media that has reported overwhelmingly negative stories about him for three years, [President Trump’s] polling numbers are at their highest ever. Why? Because the president kept his promise to remember the forgotten, and he’s continued to push ahead with policies to help them and every American,” writes Heritage Foundation President Kay Coles James in [_The Washington Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b3aec2f63a&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

To build on that success in 2019, we have exciting news to share with you today: **the Serve America Victory Fund is back for 2020!** Last month, when Seth and our team relaunched the Serve America PAC, we endorsed nine new House candidates—all impressive, inspiring leaders with military backgrounds serving our country. This cycle, we know we need to keep the House blue and expand our majority. These nine candidates—and more endorsees to come—will help us do exactly that. name: Team Collins
subject: Making sure you saw this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Collins for Senator
body:
Hey Team! We wanted to flag an op-ed for you this morning that discusses Senator Collins' credentials and dedication to Maine.
To unsubscribe click [here](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=NDE3NTc2OTYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMDk0NTA5NwS2&mt=1&rt=0)

[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=NDE3NTc2OTYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMDk0NTA5NwS2&mt=1&rt=0)
_****_
Hey Team! We wanted to flag an op-ed for you this morning that discusses Senator Collins' credentials and dedication to Maine.
The election ahead is not going to be easy, but Senator Collins is a fighter and she's going to run a strong, successful campaign!
The article is below, and we hope after you read it you will forward this to your family and friends across Maine!
Thanks for all of your support! Collins 2020!
\- Team Collins

* * *
**
OPINION: A balanced look at what Sen. Collins offers Maine**
_Mainers should value her work on "close-to-home" issues._
[KJ/MS](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=NDE3NTc2OTYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMDk0NTA5NwS2&mt=1&rt=0) | Robert Charles _grew up in Wayne, and served in the Reagan and George H.W. Bush White Houses, as a naval intelligennce officer, and as assistant secretary of state to Colin Powell. He is often in Maine._
Having grown up in rural Maine, served in the military and three branches of the federal government, and with family across the state, I have a few reflections on **Sen. Susan Collins** -- now up for re-election -- that may be worth notice.
Since everyone's politics are central to how we hear them, my loyalty is America first, Maine second, party allegiance third. I served as assistant secretary of state under Colin Powell, 10 years in Naval Intelligence, and am a registered Republican.
That settled, here is what many are missing about **Collins**. Maine is a rural, aging, largely married, predominantly Christian, increasingly educated, but economically challenged state.
On the numbers, 1,345,790 people now live in the state, just under half over age 50. More than half the population is married, and 88 percent of Maine's homeowners are married. On going to college, and gaining associate degrees, BAs, and graduate degrees, women outpace men, although an overwhelming majority of Mainers graduate high school.
The average Mainer makes $36,303, but 40% of islanders live below poverty level. Although Maine's average poverty level is 12.5%, in sync with national numbers, unmarried women face a staggering 35% poverty rate.
Ten percent of Maine's population is veterans, almost half from Vietnam. More than 5,000 World War II veterans live in the state. More than twice the number of Maine vets are over 35 than under, with more than 56,000 over 54, making VA benefits important. More than a third of Maine vets are either disabled or live below the poverty line.
What does this data mean? It means Mainers should value leadership on "close to home" issues -- the economy, education, aging, health and veterans' benefits, and employment opportunities, rural access to income, fisheries, agriculture and island life to tourism.
Putting aside "tail wags dog" national issues, from impeachment and appointments to foreign policy and national security, is **Susan****Collins** focused on what really matters? When all is quiet -- if you can find a quiet spot -- here is what may be worth knowing: Maine's senior senator is not just independent, she is focused -- and has always been -- on what matters to Mainers.
Now very senior in D.C., **Collins** was raised in rural Maine, became a trusted protégé of Maine's respected former senator and America's secretary of defense, Bill Cohen. Veteran friends describe her leadership as pathbreaking on veterans' issues that matter, from VA reform to PTSD benefits.
Veterans matter to **Collins** , politics aside. One young Mainer, missing an arm at the elbow, quietly told me how **Collins** came to Walter Reed to see him. He wanted to show a slide deck of time in Iraq. No chair in the room, she sat down on the floor -- and proceeded to listen, watch, ask questions, and care for nearly an hour. Who does that in political Washington these days? She does.
In support of Maine's aging population, she is a vicar. She chairs the Senate Aging Committee, as Cohen did before her, advocating effectively for attention, benefits and critical protections. She recently alerted Mainers early of Social Security scams roiling the nation, and consistently uses her seniority to press essential benefits into the appropriations bills.
One perk of seniority in the Senate, especially when part of the majority, is assignment to powerful committees. **Collins** has not missed a beat, somehow getting on Appropriations, including subcommittees advancing agriculture, rural development, commerce, health, energy, veterans, housing and defense.
If that was not enough, she also sits on the Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee, and the subcommittee on Primary Health and Aging. To that, add a seat on the Select Committee on Intelligence. To those who argue, as Stephen King recently did, that seniority is a liability -- that is not so. While **Collins** has served long enough to be senior, her credibility inures greatly to Maine's benefit.
Finally, independence and integrity-based leadership is uncommon in Washington today. It was uncommon already when I served with Colin Powell; he personally personified it. **Collins** personifies it too.
She has broken repeatedly from Senate leadership, just as often from expectations on the left, centering herself around what she felt was right each time. Mainers should be proud of that. Is that not what we should ask of each other, and of our senators? If not that, then what? On the numbers, CQ Roll Call reports **Collins** sided with Obama 75.9% of the time, one of only two Republicans with him 70 percent of the time; she backed Obama's Supreme Court nominees, Sonia Sotomayor and Elena Kagan. By comparison, she has sided with Trump 66% of the time. In short, she is the real thing; she is her own person, something most Mainers respect.
Three other Maine senators, with whom I once talked politics, independence, courage and integrity -- the late Margaret Chase Smith, late Edmund Muskie, and former Sen. Cohen, all were cut from that cloth. Or perhaps better said, **Collins** is cut from theirs.
Smith delivered her famous "Declaration of Conscience" in 1950, stirring as much ire as respect. Muskie served as secretary of state, laboring to get America's hostages home, steering into the wind -- to no avail but with honor. Cohen led the Defense Department when asked by a president not of his party.
Mainers are distinguished by many traits, some of which show in data -- many of which do not. One is being proudly independent, stubbornly so, determined to reach decisions by turning inward not bowing to pressure. When we get a senator who does that, who raises as much ire as awe, who frustrates the mob, disabuses national parties on both sides of their view that she will be weak, we should be happy.
That is what **Collins** offers, if we pause to lay down our swords -- leadership worth noticing.

name: Andrew Yang
subject: A note from me as New Hampshire votes today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| [](http://act.yang2020.com/go/68?t=1&akid=7372%2E377263%2EjLECBs) Yang Gang --

As Granite Staters head to the polls today, I wanted to send you a note thanking you for everything you’ve done to build this campaign.

On my first visits to New Hampshire, I remember just one, two, or three people showing up for events. Now, this past weekend, every event was packed -- some with 700-800 people overflowing outside.

This movement we’ve built together truly is special, Yang Gang -- and it would not have been possible without you.

But I have to make one last ask -- can you pitch in? We missed our goal last night and it would be a big help if you could help close the gap. Here’s the link to give: [https://act.yang2020.com/go/donate](https://act.yang2020.com/go/25133?t=5)

Thank you for everything, and stay tuned for more from me tonight.

-Andrew | **We don't have corporate PACs or big donors who can close the fundraising gap we face, just grassroots donors like you powering this campaign. Please give now.** | [Contribute](https://act.yang2020.com/go/25133?t=6&akid=7372%2E377263%2EjLECBs)

name: Ed Markey
subject: Add your name to tell Congress to reject Trump's budget and protect Social Security and Medicare
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:
Donald Trump released his proposed budget yesterday, and it once again put his disdain for the most vulnerable Americans on full display.
# [](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1de7e/693b8b0a/432b46d2/72de4f1d/3317354299/VEsH/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-em-2020?refcode=em_header")

Friends -
Donald Trump released his proposed budget yesterday, and it once again put his disdain for the most vulnerable Americans on full display.
His budget is immoral, indefensible, and unacceptable.
Donald Trump is proposing cuts to critical programs for working families -- all aimed at ending any sort of safety net for the American people. Meanwhile, he's proposing massive increases in defense spending.
Three days ago, he was promising not to touch Medicare or Social Security, but that was yet another lie. His budget shows that he clearly wants to make cuts and reduce these benefits.
We should be expanding these programs -- not making cuts to pay for Republicans' tax breaks for the wealthy.
I need your help to make sure Congress protects these benefits for all. Will you add your voice to mine today?
[**Tell Congress: Do not allow Donald Trump to cut Medicare and Social Security spending. The American people should not have to give up their health care or retirement to pay for tax cuts for corporations and the 1%.**](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1de7e/693b8b0a/432b46d2/72de4f1c/3317354299/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAyMTEtdDIifQ%3D%3D/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://markey.bluestatedigital.com/page/signup/protect-medicare-and-social-security?source=em200211-t2")
Donald Trump's tax giveaway to the ultra-rich blew a trillion-dollar hole in the deficit. We will not allow Medicare and Social Security to be cut to pay for it.
All my best,
Ed Markey
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1de7e/693b8b0a/432b46d2/72de4f1c/3317354299/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAyMTEtdDIifQ%3D%3D/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://markey.bluestatedigital.com/page/signup/protect-medicare-and-social-security?source=em200211-t2")**

* * *
Paid for by The Markey Committee
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1de7e/693b8b0a/432b46d2/72de4f13/3317354299/VEsC/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "http://ma.edmarkey.com/unsubscribe") if you'd like to unsubscribe. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email. To support Senator Ed Markey with a contribution, [click here](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1de7e/693b8b0a/432b46d2/72de4f12/3317354299/VEsD/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-em-2020?refcode=em_footer").

Now we're focused on flipping the US Senate, defeating 25 climate deniers in Congress and flipping key state legislatures across the country.

Friend, This week, I'm attending nine public events across the district, and I invite you to bring your friends and family! **February 11- Texas Star Women Candidate Forum** 11:30am - 1:00pm **Diamond Oaks Country Club** 5821 Diamond Oaks Dr N. Fort Worth, TX 76117 **February 11th - Wise County Chamber of Commerce Candidate Forum** 6:30 PM - 7:30 PM **Decatur Conference Center** 2010 US-380 Decatur, TX 76234 **February 12th - Fort Worth Coffee with Chris** 8:00 AM - 10:00 AM **Panera Bread** 4611 S Hulen St Fort Worth, TX 76132 **February 12th - Benbrook Happy Hour** 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM **3 Parrots Taco Shop** 8737 Benbrook Blvd Benbrook, TX 76126 **February 13th - Tarrant County Town Hall Meeting** 7:00 PM - 8:00 PM **R.D. Evans Community Center** 242 Lackland Rd Fort Worth, TX 76116
**February 15th - Block Walk for Chris Putnam** 9:30 AM **Roots Coffeehouse** 9101 TX-26 North Richland Hills, TX 76180 _Email cody@putnamfortexas.com for details. _ **February 16th - Teitelbaum Meet & Greet ** 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM 556 TrailRider Road Fort Worth, TX 76114 _See[calendar](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/z6892Prjcr4763lvfJKtWfQq6w/f6nr2vJKbOzdX4gyCdH9iQ/TRSo85ZUPWC892OWjQv7z763lQ) event for gate code!_ **February 16th - Parker County Coffee with Chris** 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM **Drake 's Yoke** 225 Shops Blvd #101 Willow Park, TX 76008 **February 16th - Rooney Meet & Greet** 6:00 PM - 7:00 PM 460 Sagebrush DR. Aledo 76008 I'm looking forward to sharing our positive message with as many people as possible before our primary election on March 3rd. name: Joe Biden
subject: Peter, I'm coming to you directly:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
I'm going to ask you to make your first donation. |
---
Peter,

In just a minute, I'm going to ask you to make your first donation, but first, let me explain why it's so important.

Today is the New Hampshire Primary Election. I'm so proud of our team that has given everything they've got to this race, and now we'll see how this turns out. Peter, no matter what, this won't be the be-all or end-all for my campaign, but it is an important step on the way to the Democratic nomination.

Peter, there has never been a better time to make your first donation to this campaign. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters, and we need to be prepared for whatever comes next.

We set a goal to raise $750,000 by tonight, and we still need a lot more donations if we're going to get there. Your donation today will send a message to the press, our opponents, Donald Trump, and anyone else who has tried to write us off that this campaign is strong and not going anywhere.

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: [Petition] Never again: no more partisan impeachment cover ups
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f64c/6cd27893/53196963/15865e66/1185021106/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | The recent impeachment trial was a farce: when we were tasked with trying Donald Trump's impeachment, Mitch McConnell and Senate Republicans denied us access to witnesses and documents. Instead of a full and fair trial, America got a partisan cover-up. **That's why I'm introducing a new rule in the Senate that would fix this issue and guarantee both the prosecution and defense a chance to call necessary witnesses and subpoena relevant documents.** [**Sign the petition and tell the Senate: future impeachment trials need witnesses and evidence.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f64c/6cd27893/53196963/15865e67/1185021106/VEsE/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://act.democracyforamerica.com/sign/em-200211-merkley-impeachment-jm/") If and when another president and another Congress face this issue, we must guarantee that the process is impartial and thorough. Unlike what happened with Donald Trump's trial, the Senate should be a place where the nation's gravest issues like impeachment are vigorously debated and thoughtfully considered -- not swept under the rug by a highly-partisan majority desperate to protect someone who has clearly committed a crime. Or perhaps worse, a highly-partisan majority determined to impeach a President by blocking evidence of innocence. That means witnesses. That means documents. That means access to _all_ of the facts -- no matter how uncomfortable they are. [**Sign the petition: join me and Democracy for America in urging the Senate to fix impeachment for the future.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f64c/6cd27893/53196963/15865e67/1185021106/VEsF/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://act.democracyforamerica.com/sign/em-200211-merkley-impeachment-jm/") _Jeff_ [Sign the Petition](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f64c/6cd27893/53196963/15865e67/1185021106/VEsC/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://act.democracyforamerica.com/sign/em-200211-merkley-impeachment-jm/") |

name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: The kitchen sink
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
Hi -- Democrats have a real shot at winning tough races across the country and flipping the Senate blue this year. And winning in Colorado -- where my friend John Hickenlooper is running for Senate -- will be critical to making that happen. I know we can win this race and flip this seat, but it's not going to be easy. The Republican Party has set their sights on Colorado and you can bet they'll throw everything but the kitchen sink at John. We can't let them get away with buying this election. **I 'll be honest: If Democrats want to take back the Senate this year, Colorado is a must-win. clean_body: | Hi -- Democrats have a real shot at winning tough races across the country and flipping the Senate blue this year. And winning in Colorado -- where my friend John Hickenlooper is running for Senate -- will be critical to making that happen. I know we can win this race and flip this seat, but it's not going to be easy. The Republican Party has set their sights on Colorado and you can bet they'll throw everything but the kitchen sink at John. We can't let them get away with buying this election. I 'll be honest: If Democrats want to take back the Senate this year, Colorado is a must-win. So I'm asking you to make a donation of $10 or more now to help John flip this Senate seat blue: If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | "=" "=" | "=" "=" | "=" "=" Ever since being elected mayor of Denver, John has worked tirelessly to protect our public lands and fight climate change. When he was governor, Colorado became the first state in the entire country to limit methane emissions. Throughout his entire career, John has fought to do right by the people of Colorado. That's how I know he'll make a great senator, not just for Colorado, but for the entire country. The stakes have never been higher, team. If we want to protect our public lands, combat climate change, expand health care and more, Democrats need to take back the Senate. John's race is critical to doing just that, so I'm asking: Will you donate $10 or more right now toward John 's campaign to help him flip this seat blue and put Democrats on the path to the majority? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | "=" "=" | "=" "=" | "=" "=" Thanks for all you do, Amy Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado P.O. Box 18886, Denver, CO 80218 ""

subject: We need to be honest with you:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
The next couple of days are going to be BUSY.
|
---

| | We've reached an important moment in our campaign -- the last FEC deadline before the primary is just over 24 hours away, and the day after that, early voting begins for the North Carolina primary. [_**There 's never been a better time to get involved. Our race is drawing a lot of attention.
North Carolina's 6th is a huge 2020 pick-up opportunity, and it has the potential to determine whether we will be able to hang on to the Democratic House Majority.
It's going to be a tough fight to the finish line.
[_**CHIP IN BEFORE THE DEADLINE ->**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d3a5/3d199a51/2f1be7de/7364ea78/2111635262/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxNzQyNiIsImUzdHpiSFZuZlgwPSI6ImttZTIwMDIxMSIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJtIn0%3D/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kme200211?refcode=kme200211_m_other&refcode2=17426")
We have SIX opponents -- four primary opponents and two Republicans.
**Our opponents are going to be among the first to look at our numbers after the FEC deadline. They 're going to see if we have what it takes to compete in this crowded, highly competitive race.**
[_**Kathy isn 't taking a dime from corporate PACs. We'll need to muster all of our grassroots strength to hit our goal before the FEC deadline. Will you chip in?**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d3a5/3d199a51/2f1be7de/7364ea78/2111635262/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxNzQyNiIsImUzdHpiSFZuZlgwPSI6ImttZTIwMDIxMSIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJtIn0%3D/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kme200211?refcode=kme200211_m_other&refcode2=17426")
Thanks,
Team Kathy

body:
We’re gearing up for a new phase of our campaign, and we need to reach out to the people across Montana. 97% of the 7000+ contributions to this campai | | | [](http://links.wilmotcollins.com/u/click?_t=9d604abc9de24d9a90a97f1ff916f905&_m=83cb6a8a806a4c79973ecdc8d33bb10c&_e=crZNQD7FllTFjypJMjH4IDPSWvWtNa0G-thrM9pIscDp5oml61sX7B6PlIh7pY56QaAMRmEaEuWStPyvnBRJYAqNaYXCnqiP6SICm12OzhH25zCS08W2agNVDbkALkdkiOHFNpy3GA5SqV3WgBrRwPn9k_axv6yAAsOmE1HDYHms9TYwb2Q0zcMRqH5SBeILYD-jlR4a1tSUOxJK-C1soQ7-8bWFE8jqnr8UOJdjO5aNSdgY8dzdysUbOMXZ3CcFIBURutuq2N3LR496p11vDLjXR8U02HKPzVt1PdqQyiJsQ9GQLpiNXuqvpV2_ZOuYRFggDBUZBBEBm6ptDyMFy8G3GuZyEf_3pWQMfglv6QhoFoaGkehQqWR8h_1o01xQbrwaBzjmyTN3Y5S4b7aTIZk8KUaHq-I-gG5d9XQvuhFApis568SfZWGUWnQTFoCd5VqilJxJnZBjlY3eQUuGARy6sDtgP3EkRJEPm2d8Ctk%3D) We’re gearing up for a new phase of our campaign, and we need to reach out to the people across Montana. 97% of the 7000+ contributions to this campaign are grassroots donors which means every other aspect of this campaign needs to be 97% grassroots as well. That means people talking to people, not fancy ad buys. We need to retire Mitch McConnell as the Majority Leader in the Senate. And we need your help to do it. Volunteer for Wilmot today by using this link: [**https://secure.wilmotcollins.com/beta/petition/2002-wc-volunteer**](http://links.wilmotcollins.com/u/click?_t=9d604abc9de24d9a90a97f1ff916f905&_m=83cb6a8a806a4c79973ecdc8d33bb10c&_e=crZNQD7FllTFjypJMjH4IBzlYgjGYkVWBIcrZ2Ojarrw4HV7-WinxAqjd3bAvMsFmIcX-Bd_JZEw6kw-4fnl2dKCG9R7XGs-r5XJO1ujnXAe5Iyr1W_yuI7NF2C7GaWSnSRo1klf1gkFjkK48qvjPHmOuvPjCix2wNhlyJmHTv2KnLE4Ci_gjsui7ssXUS0nOKBsPqgur8ERf_Hf79yjz_hS2cO9UOepoMKK9gnwg7i7WvNELcvwqFJFomzgc7K4cxapZmuaMj_GCMs3cwsvPi4LWB7-O98SylsE20zx63K1MSCFSaaYY738pV1eOb-H) [**And if you’re too busy to volunteer or not local, you can always donate to support our many hardworking volunteers. We recommend a donation of $30 to help us outfit one volunteer shift.**](http://links.wilmotcollins.com/u/click?_t=9d604abc9de24d9a90a97f1ff916f905&_m=83cb6a8a806a4c79973ecdc8d33bb10c&_e=crZNQD7FllTFjypJMjH4IDPSWvWtNa0G-thrM9pIscDp5oml61sX7B6PlIh7pY56QaAMRmEaEuWStPyvnBRJYAqNaYXCnqiP6SICm12OzhH25zCS08W2agNVDbkALkdkiOHFNpy3GA5SqV3WgBrRwPn9k_axv6yAAsOmE1HDYHms9TYwb2Q0zcMRqH5SBeILYD-jlR4a1tSUOxJK-C1soQ7-8bWFE8jqnr8UOJdjO5aNSdgY8dzdysUbOMXZ3CcF6dfChJ2U6YMoZqOm1JjGOlPU7C5Vf9za10eYuxbcPnhFApmXRsnURXXrzscQ4XdVUYr3_iH7sjlOKjO05OQnv5sLYv8gXZY3Ejd_f-CsbAE_zONF3mCftD9Ls9NE10QXt-mpA9pzArOTz2ezH2tqf-WUEh9F_4oNQTavLlAWiOXvelqoXmg9bV1HezRl0j_1ARFgrokovU85Uu6XZiUSeTAhBkd5bIL91XTpKoR2WHr-az8JQqOMyKB8SJoa0LMa) Thanks for all your hard work, Trent Bolger
Campaign Manager
Wilmot Collins for U.S. Senate

Wilmot Collins is Montana’s first black mayor, a 20+ year military Veteran, a refugee and now, a U.S. Senate candidate. Wilmot will prioritize fighting climate change, making healthcare a human right, economic opportunity, supporting our veterans, improving our public schools and keeping public lands in public hands. name: James Mackler
subject: RE: Social Security & Medicare cuts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa181/18d6a294/4cc6836a/26d62f73/3983485001/VEsH/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | **The Trump Administration already proposed cutting billions from Social Security, and now, the president 's budget says he'll cut Medicare, too.** [_**Speak out: Add your name and oppose dangerous cuts to Social Security and Medicare. ->**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa181/18d6a294/4cc6836a/26d62f70/3983485001/VEsE/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e200211p_m") The president has falsely promised time and time again to protect Social Security and Medicare. His budget released this week also includes sharp reductions for children's health insurance, environmental protection, and ending the public service student loan repayment program. **Here 's the truth: President Trump's policies have made life harder for Tennesseans.** Not only has Trump threatened the future of Social Security and Medicare, he's already tried to take away health care from thousands of hardworking Tennesseans at a time when our state struggles with rampant rural hospital closures and the opioid epidemic. Trump isn't looking out for us. I'm in this race to fight for all Tennesseans -- plain and simple. [_**Add your name to mine and condemn Trump 's attacks on Social Security and Medicare.**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa181/18d6a294/4cc6836a/26d62f70/3983485001/VEsF/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e200211p_m") Thank you, James
---

|

name: Tom Steyer
subject: The DNC isn’t listening
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()
body:
| | | |
---
---
Friend, I was extremely disappointed to see that the Democratic National Committee has narrowed the polling window significantly in order to qualify for the upcoming debate in Las Vegas. Right now, only polls conducted during the DNC’s tight timeframe qualify — that excludes any polling from Nevada or South Carolina. If the window for polls had started just one week prior, our campaign would have qualified because of the incredible support we’ve seen in those states — 15% in South Carolina and 12% in Nevada. Voters across the states deserve to be heard in this process, and to narrow the debate requirements in a way that excludes their voices. [_**Join me in calling on the DNC to expand the polling timeframe for the upcoming debate to make it consistent with previous polling windows, allow for more early state polls, and enable more candidates to qualify for the debate.**_](http://clicks.tomsteyer.com/ls/click?upn=M5l2K6CF-2BTeSi2jZtEX6WDq0JgL4SZ9fws6CW-2B-2BU1P0rESEmzq-2BsudDEFObKjbZqV3evp-2FyOx2cP6IIheElu8CfA1mBS26akfbslqUMZOf08VtLY9-2FYzAPPbX2CcMvEPv5ZNbrsNzMm-2FaU3drEKgWxFyZ1S1cyHGKsJipEzZyxcqXveB1Ssa5b0zvYYyQt5NwTohaAWHttQcgkwjSHsMSA-3D-3DVkZ9_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymjI06VISPfJIPkjxFld-2BrrlsLfWBgSznyC9eEQeuE67ByW7-2F4rW5t238CYEo3QD0YfZDSp62LY6ZW0GbvTjHrcTY8NJKCHSJXxRKPQ6DxtSLqKNcLXolb7KHRUTHkKFLBejNvwziS6KL34l-2BVuK8pL4oFMoUzBKyLsj6O4NP4h6M8N7QH73UPzfNjFhdqsi459BFJd81mcn0YRnN3I9-2FgyxDGF1cyoaQTAKtneGUvY4Ul-2FfwAJYZwWdDM8vBW-2BsCDBCTJXPCiBLOjkggBJOYy05ygcGwl-2FIw2Vw-2BogQqpjHa6Jtbzl9iimh7S05F5Mo5kjTpyAsGTYI9SVJ4y4JVr43a06rlRqVDCzGdAAy9I6kHOsPCPyQ3ssylD5f8svzJEEhfo6sgpQILov2zBt7j3bJRTnmcHXMSSbNyFmKsueS42ycjO1yamsssh90sfJVrik-3D) This is not the time to create a false choice — Americans deserve to hear from all the candidates so they can decide who the best person is to lead our country and defeat Donald Trump this November. Onward,

clean_body: | | | | Friend, I was extremely disappointed to see that the Democratic National Committee has narrowed the polling window significantly in order to qualify for the upcoming debate in Las Vegas. Right now, only polls conducted during the DNC’s tight timeframe qualify — that excludes any polling from Nevada or South Carolina. If the window for polls had started just one week prior, our campaign would have qualified because of the incredible support we’ve seen in those states — 15% in South Carolina and 12% in Nevada. Voters across the states deserve to be heard in this process, and to narrow the debate requirements in a way that excludes their voices. This is not the time to create a false choice — Americans deserve to hear from all the candidates so they can decide who the best person is to lead our country and defeat Donald Trump this November. Onward, Tom | | | "Facebook " | "Twitter " | "Instagram " ---|---|--- | Thank you for being a part of this campaign! We want to make sure you're receiving relevant information about how you can help us do what's right. If you'd like to learn more about volunteering, . name: Texas Battleground HQ
subject: Mitch McConnell doesn't want you to read this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
He's desperate to keep a lid on our enthusiasm so his right-hand man, Sen. John Cornyn, can sail to reelection.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905f0/7f51f7bc/4d2a8290/4ef04eeb/2791712418/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter --

If Mitch McConnell knew how many people are reading this email right now, he would _flip out!_ With just **7 days before early voting begins in the Texas primary** , he's _desperate_ to keep a lid on our enthusiasm so his right-hand man, Sen. John Cornyn, can sail to reelection. | [](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905f0/7f51f7bc/4d2a8290/4ef04eea/2791712418/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjkwMSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-febfec?refcode=email_20200211.2nd__901__&amount=10&recurring=true")
---
**To make sure that doesn't happen, we need MJ to be the one taking him on. So we need you to get engaged at this critical moment![Will you make your first* donation ahead of tomorrow's filing deadline to help us reach our $75,000 goal?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905f0/7f51f7bc/4d2a8290/4ef04eea/2791712418/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjkwMSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-febfec?refcode=email_20200211.2nd__901__&amount=10&recurring=true")**
**[**DONATE NOW**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905f0/7f51f7bc/4d2a8290/4ef04eea/2791712418/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjkwMSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-febfec?refcode=email_20200211.2nd__901__&amount=10&recurring=true")**
Running a winning campaign is expensive -- _especially_ in Texas -- and that's exactly why Mitch McConnell is hoping you ignore emails like this one. He knows we don't have an army of special interests and corporate PACs lined up like Sen. Cornyn does. **[And that means if we're going to win, it'll be on the strength of grassroots supporters like you.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905f0/7f51f7bc/4d2a8290/4ef04eea/2791712418/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjkwMSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-febfec?refcode=email_20200211.2nd__901__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

Sen. Cornyn has over $12 million in his war chest, and he's already used it to run the first TV ads of the race. Plus, the Koch network has been spending big for him!

**[Every donation from this team](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905f0/7f51f7bc/4d2a8290/4ef04eea/2791712418/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjkwMSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-febfec?refcode=email_20200211.2nd__901__&amount=10&recurring=true") goes straight toward fighting back against their big money and breaking McConnell's stranglehold on our government.** That means helping MJ travel to every corner of the state, talk to _thousands_ of Texans about the issues affecting their families, and spread her message about a new Senate that works for ALL of us.

**Please rush a donation now to help us reach our $75,000 Filing Deadline Goal and give MJ the resources to BEAT Sen. Cornyn:**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

| Hey, Friends - [can you chip in $5 to Michael Bennet's campaign?](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbaa58/134673f2/371926a8/699737f3/1148797139/VEsH/?g=GUSlGlH8x8Vcy9-bgCeB4mA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bfa-feb?refcode=EM_FR_B3_021120_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=10,29,50,100,250,500,1000")

name: Team Kennedy
subject: All the President's lies
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kennedy for Massachusetts
body:
| [](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390307/55b66746/22cefbfb/80fe750/339434422/VEsH/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "http://www.kennedyforma.com/")
---

| | **Last week:****** The President stood in front of Congress and told the nation he would not make any cuts to our social safety net. **On Saturday:****** He tweeted he would not touch Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security. **Then yesterday:** He proposed a budget that would cut billions from essential programs - including significant changes to Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security. Luckily, we know this insidious, cruel budget will NOT pass the House. **But we need to make our values and our positions clear.[ Sign your name, tell Donald Trump that in no circumstance should we cut funding to programs like Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security. ](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390307/55b66746/22cefbfb/80fe751/339434422/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/petition/protect-social-programs/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2020-02-protect-social-programs-email")** [PROTECT OUR SOCIAL SAFETY NET](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390307/55b66746/22cefbfb/80fe751/339434422/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/petition/protect-social-programs/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2020-02-protect-social-programs-email") Joe will do everything he can to protect our social safety net - in his current position in the House and, if elected, in the Senate. We can't leave the most vulnerable among us without health care, and we can not and will not budge on protecting Social Security, ever. No cuts. Team Kennedy [Sign our Petition](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390307/55b66746/22cefbfb/80fe751/339434422/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/petition/protect-social-programs/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2020-02-protect-social-programs-email") |

name: Chris Putnam
subject: Only three weeks left..
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PUTNAM FOR CONGRESS [Unsubscribe](http://sendy
body:
|
---
Friend, In less than 21 days, all of our hard work will finally be rewarded as we replace Kay Granger with a TRUE conservative. Over the years, **we have seen Kay Granger become more like Nancy Pelosi by voting for increased deficits, funding for Planned Parenthood, big government bailouts, and amnesty for illegal immigrants. ** I'm running for Congress to fight for the people of Texas' 12th congressional district, and to provide President Trump with a reliable partner to Keep America Great. **Kay Granger knows she 's in trouble and is doing whatever it takes to drown out our message on the airwaves.** While she may have the support of the political establishment behind her, **I 'm confident I will earn the vote of the people of Texas this March 3rd.** With time running out, it's important we keep our eyes focused on the finish line and waste no time these next few weeks. [**Will you help me reach voters across the district by chipping in $21 right now?**](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/z6892Prjcr4763lvfJKtWfQq6w/haxcx892Edlu4LtMqlDWTijA/KmwKQejnLJjg892jT763pyGJ5A) [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/z6892Prjcr4763lvfJKtWfQq6w/haxcx892Edlu4LtMqlDWTijA/KmwKQejnLJjg892jT763pyGJ5A) [**If we act now, Kay Granger and the establishment have no chance of stopping us. But that means I need you to donate $21 or more _today_. **](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/z6892Prjcr4763lvfJKtWfQq6w/haxcx892Edlu4LtMqlDWTijA/KmwKQejnLJjg892jT763pyGJ5A) With supporters like you on my side, I like our chances this March.
\- Chris

---
While our team is working hard to get out the vote in New Hampshire, I wanted to share a memo with you on what's next after today's election.

We have a plan to organize in all 57 states and territories across the country, continue to meet people where they are, talk with them about the issues that matter to them, and spread Elizabeth's vision for big, structural change.

We're confident that if we all step up and do our part, this plan is our path to victory.

Before we dive into the details, I do want to note that this campaign has always been 100% grassroots-funded, and that's never going to change. [**Grassroots supporters like you are the only people who make this plan to victory possible. Can Elizabeth count on you to chip in today to help power our movement forward?**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ae2a/45c30650/1f9870b87/66e280d8/1380240087/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWZlYjIwZnItbWFyY2gzIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTU82MEZfTU1PX1NUUl9RXzIwMjAwMjExX1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-feb20fr-march3?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMO60F_MMO_STR_Q_20200211_V1S3_P") | _If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_

**WHAT WE LEARNED FROM IOWA**

With no clear winner out of Iowa, the race for the Democratic nomination is a fractured one. And the remaining three early states of New Hampshire, Nevada, and South Carolina appear poised to keep the race wide open.

No candidate has come close yet to receiving majority support among the Democratic primary electorate, and there is no candidate that has yet shown the ability to consolidate support. As noted by Politico this morning, "There is no candidate who can deliver the kind of knockout punch that defined some previous Democratic primary races, such as Al Gore's 2000 win and John Kerry's 2004 win, when they each won Iowa and New Hampshire and never looked back."

People who are predicting what will happen a week from now are the same people who a year ago predicted that Beto O'Rourke was a frontrunner for the nomination. Barely over a week ago, a fifth place finish in Iowa was seen likely to knock Amy Klobuchar out of the race, and much of the media and pundit class predicted Pete Buttigieg's fade in the Iowa caucuses. As we've seen in the last week, debates and unexpected results have an outsize impact on the race, and will likely keep it volatile and unpredictable through Super Tuesday.

As I laid out in my last memo, we've built an organization to match what we expect to be a drawn-out contest to accumulate delegates everywhere.

After New Hampshire tonight, 98% of pledged delegates will still be up for grabs. And as the race consolidates after Super Tuesday, we expect the results to show that **Elizabeth Warren is the consensus choice of the widest coalition of Democrats in every corner of the country.**

One key statistic out of Iowa that illustrates the breadth of Warren's appeal: On the second alignment, which shows the choices that voters make as candidates are eliminated, **Elizabeth won the most votes in both the _bluest_ county in Iowa _and_ the _reddest_ county in Iowa:** Johnson County, which voted 65% for Hillary Clinton in the 2016 general election, and Sioux County, which voted just 12.5% for Clinton in 2016.

Our campaign has been organizing in traditionally red and blue areas of Nevada, South Carolina and states voting in March for months, and in some places nearly a year, and we are confident that we'll continue to show strength by competing everywhere, not just in pockets that reflect one segment of our party or another.

**BUILDING ON NEW HAMPSHIRE**

Our New Hampshire operation has picked up steam at just the right time. This past Saturday, after **a strong debate performance and our campaign's best debate-day fundraising to date** , Elizabeth kicked off a record-setting canvass with close to 1,000 volunteers heading straight to the doors.

Through Sunday night, just halfway through our four-day Get-Out-The-Vote window, our organization had already hit 138% of our GOTV goal for calls and door knocks with over 3,500 people taking out canvass packets.

As voters head to the polls today, we're confident we'll be leaving it all on the field. Our organization is fired up, and we're just getting started.

**LOOKING AHEAD**

Every one of the Democratic candidates remaining would make a better president than Donald Trump. But the primary contests ahead will be a test for all of the campaigns. Our opponents each tell the story of their strengths, but it's worth a quick, sober look at the landscape of their challenges:

Senator Sanders starts with a ceiling that's significantly lower than the support he had four years ago. In Iowa, Sanders got no more than half the number of people who caucused for him last time, despite spending $11.2 million in the state on TV ads -- a third more than he spent in 2016. In New Hampshire, he is on track to receive around half of his 2016 vote share as well. And he hasn't yet faced the scrutiny of his record that will surely come with any further rise.

Former Vice President Biden entered this race as the clear frontrunner, reaching over 40% in national polling last spring. He's now polling under 30% even among older voters and African-American voters, who have been his strongest supporters, and his support among younger voters has fallen to just 6%.

Former Mayor Buttigieg's most significant challenge is yet to come, as the contest moves into states with more diverse electorates, and he still hasn't answered tough questions about his record in South Bend. And as the results in Iowa showed, according to Iowa's Starting Line, "Buttigieg's numbers in Waterloo, the Iowa city with the highest percentage of African Americans, were brutal."

Former Mayor Bloomberg has poured hundreds of millions of dollars into the March states, but is on track to fall short of winning delegates in 85% of Super Tuesday districts. And keep in mind that Bloomberg will soon be forced to actually debate his record, rather than hiding behind millions in TV ads.

Senator Klobuchar is getting a well-deserved look from voters for the first time, but hasn't been able to build out infrastructure for the long haul, and is playing catch up on a very short timeline. Like others in the race, she hasn't yet faced the tough questions and scrutiny that accompany rising momentum.

After today, the primary race moves to two of the most diverse contests in the country: Nevada and South Carolina. Through today, our organization is closing in on nearly a million contacts with voters in each state. And let's not forget that early voting has already begun in many states.

When the race moves on from the early states, votes for Warren will already have been cast in 19 states with election days that follow South Carolina. And within 72 hours of the South Carolina polls closing, Super Tuesday results will be rolling in.

**SUPER TUESDAY**
[](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ae2a/45c30650/1f9870b87/66e280cf/1380240087/VEsEDQ/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiI0NTcwNTY0IiwiSlNWbGJXRnBiQ1VsIjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IkVNTzYwRl9NTU9fU1RSX1FfMjAyMDAyMTFfVjFTM19QIn0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "http://mi.elizabethwarren.com/p/cp/04c58ef4b6faab98/c?mi_u=4570564&mi_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMO60F_MMO_STR_Q_20200211_V1S3_P&url=http%3A%2F%2Fmi.elizabethwarren.com%2Fp%2Frp%2F58f223adada8e337%2Furl")
Right on the heels of South Carolina, there are more than 150 congressional or other districts at stake on Super Tuesday. To be eligible for a majority of the delegates in the race for the nomination, candidates must meet or exceed a 15% threshold at the congressional district level (and at the state Senate district level in Texas).

**Our internal projections show us at or above the 15% threshold in 108 of those districts, or nearly two-thirds of the Super Tuesday map.** If you broaden that to districts where we're within reach of the threshold (or between 12-15%), **we're playing in 149 districts, or 88% of the map.**

**Warren is poised to finish in the top two in over half of Super Tuesday states (eight of 14), in the top three in all of them, and is on pace to pick up at-large statewide delegates in all but one.**

Despite being in the race for a year and effectively tied for second in Iowa, Pete Buttigieg fails to break the 15% threshold in any of the Super Tuesday states and is in danger of getting locked out of critical statewide delegates entirely. The same is true of Mike Bloomberg, despite already spending more than $600 million of his own money on the race, having an unlimited budget, and a footprint of more than 2,000 staff.

Our internal projections show the other candidates at these threshold rates:

Total districts in play on Super Tuesday: 165
* **Sanders at or above threshold in 161 districts (98%)**
* **Biden at or above threshold in 159 districts (96%)**
* **Warren at or above threshold in 108 districts (65%)**
* Bloomberg at or above threshold in 25 districts (15%)
* Buttigieg at or above threshold in 10 districts (6%)
* Klobuchar at or above threshold in Minnesota only (5%)
* No other candidate is currently projected to reach the 15% threshold in a single Super Tuesday district

This means that Super Tuesday is shaping up to be a significant winnowing contest on this field. By the end of the day on March 3, 38% of total delegates to the national convention will have been awarded.

And by the first week of April, two-thirds of delegates will be awarded. **At this point, our numbers show only three candidates at or above the 15% threshold in more than half of districts: Joe Biden, Bernie Sanders, and Elizabeth Warren.**

The road to the Democratic nomination is not paved with statewide winner-take-all victories. This is not a race for governor, the U.S. Senate or the state treasurer's office. This is a district-by-district contest for pledged delegates awarded proportionally.

It's not a straightforward narrative captured by glancing at a map, and the process won't be decided by the simple horse race numbers in clickbait headlines. That's never been our focus -- our focus is on building a broad coalition to win delegates everywhere.

We're confident in our plan and our path because we know the truth at the heart of the nomination process: no amount of spin and hyperventilation can change the delegate math, and no amount of money can buy a candidate his way back into this race if he can't play for serious delegates on Super Tuesday.

If the early states deliver mixed results for the field, and no seismic event shakes up the top three, the remaining viable candidates for the Democratic nomination as of Super Tuesday will be Joe Biden, Bernie Sanders, and Elizabeth Warren. **In that three-way race, Elizabeth Warren is the candidate with the highest potential ceiling of support and the one best positioned to unite the party and lead the Democratic ticket to defeat Donald Trump.**

For months, Warren has been near or at the top of the list of candidates that voters are considering for president. According to the latest _Economist/YouGov_ poll, 40-50% of voters across all age groups are considering voting for Warren, including 58% of self-described liberals and 38% of self-described moderates -- giving Warren more support from moderates than any candidate besides Biden.

And in the event that the former VP Biden's support collapses before South Carolina and Super Tuesday, as some observers predict, his support splinters among the other candidates -- including Sanders and Warren. Since the She the People forum last year in Texas, where Warren received a rare standing ovation, through last week's debate in Manchester, where she demonstrated the depth of her long-standing focus on systemic racism across issues well beyond criminal justice reform, Warren has shown a capacity to build the kind of a broad-based, inclusive coalition necessary to win the nomination. Candidates on the outside looking in to the top three face a very steep climb.

**HELP ELIZABETH WIN**

Despite the media's assumption that voters will flock toward the candidate deemed most "electable," our numbers show that a close, three-way race will emerge over the weeks and months to come.

Elizabeth Warren is the only candidate in the top three to have never run for president before (or in Joe Biden's case, twice before). Our campaign is [**no stranger**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ae2a/45c30650/1f9870b87/66e280ce/1380240087/VEsEDA/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://www.vanityfair.com/news/2019/04/elizabeth-warren-campaign-fundraising") to being [**written off**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ae2a/45c30650/1f9870b87/66e28f31/1380240087/VEsFBQ/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://www.cnn.com/2019/04/13/politics/elizabeth-warren-poll-of-the-week/index.html") or [**counted out**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ae2a/45c30650/1f9870b87/66e28f30/1380240087/VEsFBA/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://www.washingtonpost.com/opinions/2019/04/09/why-hasnt-elizabeth-warren-achieved-liftoff/") early.

**But here's what we do know: Warren has proven the doubters wrong before.**

With a strong organization of more than 1,000 staff on the ground in 31 states, bolstered by a movement of more than 1,000,000 grassroots donors, we are confident that as Americans continue to get to know Elizabeth, they'll see what we see every day: That Warren is the only candidate who has a track record of mobilizing a national grassroots movement while also building the bridges our party needs to unite and defeat Donald Trump in November. Elizabeth is progressive, effective, and has lived a life that has prepared her for this fight.

But the only way we'll win this nomination if is every supporter reading this digs deep and funds our 100% grassroots-funded campaign. If you're ready to elect Elizabeth Warren, [**pitch in $5 or whatever you can and power this movement through the critical races to come.**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ae2a/45c30650/1f9870b87/66e280d8/1380240087/VEsFBw/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWZlYjIwZnItbWFyY2gzIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTU82MEZfTU1PX1NUUl9RXzIwMjAwMjExX1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-feb20fr-march3?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMO60F_MMO_STR_Q_20200211_V1S3_P")

We have a plan to organize in all 57 states and territories across the country, continue to meet people where they are, talk with them about the issues that matter to them, and spread Elizabeth's vision for big, structural change. We're confident that if we all step up and do our part, this plan is our path to victory. Before we dive into the details, I do want to note that this campaign has always been 100% grassroots-funded, and that's never going to change. BUILDING ON NEW HAMPSHIRE Our New Hampshire operation has picked up steam at just the right time. This past Saturday, after a strong debate performance and our campaign's best debate-day fundraising to date , Elizabeth kicked off a record-setting canvass with close to 1,000 volunteers heading straight to the doors. Through Sunday night, just halfway through our four-day Get-Out-The-Vote window, our organization had already hit 138% of our GOTV goal for calls and door knocks with over 3,500 people taking out canvass packets. As voters head to the polls today, we're confident we'll be leaving it all on the field. Our organization is fired up, and we're just getting started. LOOKING AHEAD Every one of the Democratic candidates remaining would make a better president than Donald Trump. But the primary contests ahead will be a test for all of the campaigns. Our opponents each tell the story of their strengths, but it's worth a quick, sober look at the landscape of their challenges: Senator Sanders starts with a ceiling that's significantly lower than the support he had four years ago. In Iowa, Sanders got no more than half the number of people who caucused for him last time, despite spending $11.2 million in the state on TV ads -- a third more than he spent in 2016. In New Hampshire, he is on track to receive around half of his 2016 vote share as well. And he hasn't yet faced the scrutiny of his record that will surely come with any further rise. Former Vice President Biden entered this race as the clear frontrunner, reaching over 40% in national polling last spring. He's now polling under 30% even among older voters and African-American voters, who have been his strongest supporters, and his support among younger voters has fallen to just 6%. Former Mayor Buttigieg's most significant challenge is yet to come, as the contest moves into states with more diverse electorates, and he still hasn't answered tough questions about his record in South Bend. And as the results in Iowa showed, according to Iowa's Starting Line, "Buttigieg's numbers in Waterloo, the Iowa city with the highest percentage of African Americans, were brutal." Former Mayor Bloomberg has poured hundreds of millions of dollars into the March states, but is on track to fall short of winning delegates in 85% of Super Tuesday districts. And keep in mind that Bloomberg will soon be forced to actually debate his record, rather than hiding behind millions in TV ads. Senator Klobuchar is getting a well-deserved look from voters for the first time, but hasn't been able to build out infrastructure for the long haul, and is playing catch up on a very short timeline. Like others in the race, she hasn't yet faced the tough questions and scrutiny that accompany rising momentum. After today, the primary race moves to two of the most diverse contests in the country: Nevada and South Carolina. Through today, our organization is closing in on nearly a million contacts with voters in each state. And let's not forget that early voting has already begun in many states. When the race moves on from the early states, votes for Warren will already have been cast in 19 states with election days that follow South Carolina. And within 72 hours of the South Carolina polls closing, Super Tuesday results will be rolling in. SUPER TUESDAY "" Right on the heels of South Carolina, there are more than 150 congressional or other districts at stake on Super Tuesday. To be eligible for a majority of the delegates in the race for the nomination, candidates must meet or exceed a 15% threshold at the congressional district level (and at the state Senate district level in Texas). Our internal projections show us at or above the 15% threshold in 108 of those districts, or nearly two-thirds of the Super Tuesday map. If you broaden that to districts where we're within reach of the threshold (or between 12-15%), we're playing in 149 districts, or 88% of the map. Warren is poised to finish in the top two in over half of Super Tuesday states (eight of 14), in the top three in all of them, and is on pace to pick up at-large statewide delegates in all but one. Despite being in the race for a year and effectively tied for second in Iowa, Pete Buttigieg fails to break the 15% threshold in any of the Super Tuesday states and is in danger of getting locked out of critical statewide delegates entirely. The same is true of Mike Bloomberg, despite already spending more than $600 million of his own money on the race, having an unlimited budget, and a footprint of more than 2,000 staff. Our internal projections show the other candidates at these threshold rates: Total districts in play on Super Tuesday: 165 Sanders at or above threshold in 161 districts (98%) Biden at or above threshold in 159 districts (96%) Warren at or above threshold in 108 districts (65%) Bloomberg at or above threshold in 25 districts (15%) Buttigieg at or above threshold in 10 districts (6%) Klobuchar at or above threshold in Minnesota only (5%) No other candidate is currently projected to reach the 15% threshold in a single Super Tuesday district This means that Super Tuesday is shaping up to be a significant winnowing contest on this field. By the end of the day on March 3, 38% of total delegates to the national convention will have been awarded. And by the first week of April, two-thirds of delegates will be awarded. At this point, our numbers show only three candidates at or above the 15% threshold in more than half of districts: Joe Biden, Bernie Sanders, and Elizabeth Warren. The road to the Democratic nomination is not paved with statewide winner-take-all victories. This is not a race for governor, the U.S. Senate or the state treasurer's office. This is a district-by-district contest for pledged delegates awarded proportionally. It's not a straightforward narrative captured by glancing at a map, and the process won't be decided by the simple horse race numbers in clickbait headlines. That's never been our focus -- our focus is on building a broad coalition to win delegates everywhere. We're confident in our plan and our path because we know the truth at the heart of the nomination process: no amount of spin and hyperventilation can change the delegate math, and no amount of money can buy a candidate his way back into this race if he can't play for serious delegates on Super Tuesday. If the early states deliver mixed results for the field, and no seismic event shakes up the top three, the remaining viable candidates for the Democratic nomination as of Super Tuesday will be Joe Biden, Bernie Sanders, and Elizabeth Warren. In that three-way race, Elizabeth Warren is the candidate with the highest potential ceiling of support and the one best positioned to unite the party and lead the Democratic ticket to defeat Donald Trump. For months, Warren has been near or at the top of the list of candidates that voters are considering for president. According to the latest Economist/YouGov poll, 40-50% of voters across all age groups are considering voting for Warren, including 58% of self-described liberals and 38% of self-described moderates -- giving Warren more support from moderates than any candidate besides Biden. And in the event that the former VP Biden's support collapses before South Carolina and Super Tuesday, as some observers predict, his support splinters among the other candidates -- including Sanders and Warren. Since the She the People forum last year in Texas, where Warren received a rare standing ovation, through last week's debate in Manchester, where she demonstrated the depth of her long-standing focus on systemic racism across issues well beyond criminal justice reform, Warren has shown a capacity to build the kind of a broad-based, inclusive coalition necessary to win the nomination. Candidates on the outside looking in to the top three face a very steep climb. HELP ELIZABETH WIN Despite the media's assumption that voters will flock toward the candidate deemed most "electable," our numbers show that a close, three-way race will emerge over the weeks and months to come. Elizabeth Warren is the only candidate in the top three to have never run for president before (or in Joe Biden's case, twice before). Our campaign is "" to being "" or "" early. But here's what we do know: Warren has proven the doubters wrong before.** With a strong organization of more than 1,000 staff on the ground in 31 states, bolstered by a movement of more than 1,000,000 grassroots donors, we are confident that as Americans continue to get to know Elizabeth, they'll see what we see every day: That Warren is the only candidate who has a track record of mobilizing a national grassroots movement while also building the bridges our party needs to unite and defeat Donald Trump in November. Elizabeth is progressive, effective, and has lived a life that has prepared her for this fight. But the only way we'll win this nomination if is every supporter reading this digs deep and funds our 100% grassroots-funded campaign. If you're ready to elect Elizabeth Warren, "" "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for President All content (C) 2020 Warren for President, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

name: Alex Meyer, Campaign Manager
subject: [Action requested] Can you help get Lisa on the ballot?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LISA SCHELLER FOR CONGRESS
body:
Have you signed Lisa’s petition to get her on the ballot?

Peter, Petition period ends next Tuesday, that means we only have one week
left to get Lisa on the ballot. Lisa is out meeting with voters, listening to
their concerns and ideas, spreading her winning message, and most importantly
getting as many signatures as possible. There is a lot of energy on the ground,
and we are close to our goal. But we can’t take anything for granted or leave
anything to chance. In fact, we need to work harder.

Peter, that's why I'm emailing you today. We need every signature we can get
to ensure Lisa gets on the ballot, and you can help.

Will you make a big impact in this race by signing Lisa’s ballot petition?

Do you live in the 7th congressional district? If your answer is “YES!”, then
follow this link to email me personally so we can send someone to come get your
signature on Lisa’s petition. <mailto:alex@schellerforcongress.com>

Your support has not gone unnoticed and is greatly appreciated. And in the
coming months, it will be all the more important to have you fighting with us.

See you on the trail,

Alex Meyer

Campaign Manager

Lisa Scheller for Congress

subject: looking for some input
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Franken for Iowa
body:
Days on the campaign trail are long and arduous, but Iowans are worth it. I sit down, eat with strangers who welcome me into their homes, and talk about the values we share and the ones they seek from
|
---
| |
---

| | Hi folks -- my team reached out last week, but I'm still looking for more input. Days on the campaign trail are long and arduous, but Iowans are worth it. I sit down, eat with strangers who welcome me into their homes, and talk about the values we share and the ones they seek from leaders in Washington. That's why the surveys we send out are so important to me -- hearing from every voter in Iowa is a worthy challenge, and these questionnaires are key. [_**So if you have a minute (that 's all it takes), answer five questions and send them to me. I appreciate it.**_](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f24e1/33365068/4a6d61ea/3f8be3c9/643833719/VEsH/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "http://act.frankenforiowa.org/february-supporter-survey?utm_medium=email&utm_source=mfranken&utm_content=1+-+So+if+you+have+a+minute+thatrsquos+all+i&utm_campaign=FRA_20200211_febsurveykick_em_su_all&source=FRA_20200211_febsurveykick_em_su_all") | [Take the Survey (1 minute) >>](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f24e1/33365068/4a6d61ea/3f8be3ca/643833719/VEsE/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://act.frankenforiowa.org/february-supporter-survey?utm_medium=email&utm_source=mfranken&utm_content=2+-+Take+the+Survey+1+minute+gtgt&utm_campaign=FRA_20200211_febsurveykick_em_su_all&source=FRA_20200211_febsurveykick_em_su_all")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump vs. the “experts”
body:
President Trump hosted a group of America’s governors for a policy discussion at the White House yesterday. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b423c424e9&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • February 11, 2020
---
| | |
# **President Trump vs. the “experts”**

**President Trump** hosted a group of America’s governors for a policy discussion at the White House yesterday, and the blue-collar economic boom was top on the agenda.

**Another expectations-crushing** [jobs report Friday](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a56bdd9070&e=3ee4985eea) set the tone. “Since my election, America has gained 7 million new jobs. We added 225,000 jobs in January alone,” the President said. “And a statistic that’s incredible to me is: The average unemployment that we’ve had during this 3-year period is the lowest in the history of our country. Compared to any other administration, the lowest in the history of our country.”

**🎬** **LARRY KUDLOW:**[Blue-collar, middle-class BOOM keeps going](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7639723292&e=3ee4985eea)

**While Washington** career politicians often ignore real-world results, local and state leaders know what a strong economy means for their workers. Since 2016, 28 states have reached or matched their lowest unemployment rates _ever,_ and a record 39 saw unemployment rates plummet below 4 percent.

**That’s no accident**. The difference is an administration that puts pro-growth, pro-worker policies like tax cuts, deregulation, and new trade deals front and center.

**Here are the results** , as shown in Friday’s new jobs report:
* “Experts” predicted 158,000 new jobs in January. America got _225,000_.

* Before President Trump’s election, the Congressional Budget Office projected a 5 percent unemployment rate. We’re now at _3.6 percent_.

* Unlike under the Obama Administration, workers’ wages today are growing _faster_ than their managers’ wages.

**🎬** [President Trump: Wages UP, unemployment DOWN for African Americans](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ca0c3fe481&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **WATCH: First Lady welcomes State of the Union guests**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=543bc9ad38&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **First Lady Melania Trump** joined the President in welcoming [a group of very special guests](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4bf5c155f8&e=3ee4985eea) to last week’s State of the Union address. Each one had a powerful story to share.

**On Twitter today,** the First Lady revealed a behind-the-scenes look from when she met some of these incredible patriots. “People everywhere can take inspiration from their optimism, strength and courage,” she said.

🇺🇸 [See the story behind special guest and American hero Charles McGee](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e63a7357d0&e=3ee4985eea)

**🎦****Highlights:** [Best moments from the State of the Union](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=67779f6bf8&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

Our campaign has been racking up endorsements in the district and across the country, because they believe that Monika is the candidate that will fight for our progressive values in Congress. This is a great opportunity for Democrats to flip a seat and keep control of the House.
We have 3 OPPONENTS in our March 3rd primary and a Republican to take on in November.
[Monika isn't taking anything from corporate donors or PACs, which means we need more grassroots donations to keep this campaign running. Will you chip in to help?](https://monika.bsd.net/page/m/4449d854/7dfbb003/42a54131/5527c8d9/437852657/VEsP/?g=DRs0ITjfd5ldy6_maJSnCeA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mjhfornc")
\- Team Monika
| | | Monika Johnson-Hostler is running for Congress to give the people of North Carolina's 2nd District a representative who will listen, work hard, and stay true to their word, doing what's best for the district. As the District 2 member of the Wake County school board and Executive Director of North Carolina's Coalition Against Sexual Assault, Monika knows how to lead with solutions. For Monika Johnson-Hostler, serving people is about integrity, and that's what she'll bring to Congress.
